findings.title: CWE-330: Weak random number generator in `initializeStaticFilePath()`
findings.description: java.util.Random or Math.random() used — not cryptographically secure.

findings.title: CWE-78: OS command injection in `exec()`
findings.description: Runtime.exec/ProcessBuilder with user-controlled input.
findings.suggestion: Avoid passing user input to exec/ProcessBuilder.

findings.title: CWE-798: Hardcoded credential at line 140
findings.description: Hardcoded secret value found at L140: ` + token + ...`.
findings.suggestion: Use environment variables, a secrets manager, or a configuration server.

findings.title: CWE-328: Weak cryptographic algorithm (MD5) at line 73
findings.description: `getInstance("MD5")` at L73. MD5 is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or stronger. For passwords, use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2.

findings.title: CWE-327: Weak cryptographic hash in `copy()`
findings.description: MD5 or SHA-1 used for hashing — vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or SHA-3 via MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256").

findings.title: Interprocedural taint: writeLogFile() at line 17
findings.description: Tainted argument flows to sink through `writeLogFile()`. Call at L17: `writeLogFile("ERROR", msg)`.
findings.suggestion: Review the data flow through helper methods. Ensure input validation at the entry point.

findings.title: Interprocedural taint: writeLogFile() at line 32
findings.description: Tainted argument flows to sink through `writeLogFile()`. Call at L32: `writeLogFile("ERROR", msg + "\r\n" + formatException(exception))`.
findings.suggestion: Review the data flow through helper methods. Ensure input validation at the entry point.

findings.title: Interprocedural taint: writeLogFile() at line 56
findings.description: Tainted argument flows to sink through `writeLogFile()`. Call at L56: `writeLogFile("INFO", msg)`.
findings.suggestion: Review the data flow through helper methods. Ensure input validation at the entry point.

findings.title: CWE-79: XSS via unencoded response write in `writeLogFile()`
findings.description: response.getWriter().write() outputs user-controlled data without HTML encoding.
findings.suggestion: Encode output with an HTML encoder or use a template engine that auto-escapes.

findings.title: CWE-79: XSS via response write() at line 32
findings.description: Response writer with dynamic content at L32: `System.out.println("parameter userId: " + request.getParameter("userId"))`.
findings.suggestion: HTML-encode all user-supplied data before writing to the HTTP response.

findings.title: CWE-79: XSS via response write() at line 35
findings.description: Response writer with dynamic content at L35: `System.out.println("parameter userId: " + request.getParameter("userId"))`.
findings.suggestion: HTML-encode all user-supplied data before writing to the HTTP response.

findings.title: CWE-601: Open redirect via sendRedirect in `sendRedirect()`
findings.description: HttpServletResponse.sendRedirect() is called with a URL that may be attacker-influenced.
findings.suggestion: Validate the redirect target against an allowlist or use a mapping instead of user-supplied URLs.

findings.title: CWE-330: Weak PRNG (`Random`) at line 74
findings.description: `new Random()` used at L74. `java.util.Random` is not cryptographically secure. An attacker can predict its output.
findings.suggestion: Use `java.security.SecureRandom` for security-sensitive values (tokens, session IDs, crypto keys). `new SecureRandom()` is the drop-in replacement.

findings.description: `new Random()` used at L86. `java.util.Random` is not cryptographically secure. An attacker can predict its output.
findings.suggestion: Use `java.security.SecureRandom` for security-sensitive values (tokens, session IDs, crypto keys). `new SecureRandom()` is the drop-in replacement.

findings.title: CWE-330: Weak random number generator in `randomKey()`
findings.description: java.util.Random or Math.random() used — not cryptographically secure.
